name: Authentic Traditions indicate that if a person takes a vow and discovers later that
introduction:
righteousness and common good are best served by breaking that vow then he should do so.
Expiation consists in either feeding or providing clothes for ten poor people, or setting free a
slave, or fasting for three days (see 5: 89).
